answer:
This is an extraordinarily significant finding. The agent was able to access extensive pre-existing research files. Let me compile the full synthesis.

---

# Strait of Hormuz Status & Middle East Oil Supply Disruption — June 2026

## Executive Summary

The Strait of Hormuz has been **effectively closed to commercial shipping since February 28, 2026**, following the US-Israeli "Operation Midnight Hammer" strikes on Iran that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and destroyed Iran's nuclear facilities. This is **the largest oil chokepoint disruption in modern energy history**. A ceasefire deal was announced by Trump on June 14, 2026, with a signing ceremony scheduled for June 19 in Geneva — but the deal's implementation remains uncertain as the IRGC has not endorsed it.

---

## 1. The Strait of Hormuz Closure

- **February 28, 2026**: Following US-Israeli strikes on Iran ("Operation Midnight Hammer"), Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz to all commercial shipping.
- The Strait normally handles **~25% of world seaborne oil** and **~20% of global LNG**.
- Tanker traffic dropped **~70% immediately**, then fell to near zero.
- **~2,000 ships and ~20,000 mariners from 87 countries** became stranded.
- S&P Global described this as **"the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy."**
- On **June 10–11**, the IRGC declared "complete closure" of the Strait and published expanded boundary maps extending into UAE territorial waters.

## 2. Key Military/Naval Incidents

| Date | Incident |
|------|----------|
| Feb 28 | Iran closes Strait of Hormuz in retaliation |
| Apr 13 | US imposes naval blockade of Iranian ports; 51+ vessels redirected |
| Apr 20 | US seizes Iranian container ship MV Touska in Gulf of Oman |
| May 4 | Trump announces "Project Freedom" — US naval escort operation (3 Carrier Strike Groups, 2 Marine Expeditionary Units) |
| May 4–5 | Iran attacks UAE Fujairah oil facilities (12 ballistic missiles, 3 cruise missiles, 4 drones); US claims 7 IRGC fast boats sunk |
| May 7 | US strikes Iranian military at Bandar Abbas and Qeshm Island |
| Jun 14 | Trump announces deal is "complete"; authorizes removal of naval blockade |

## 3. Oil Price & Economic Impact

| Date | Brent Crude ($/bbl) | Context |
|------|---------------------|---------|
| Feb 27 (pre-war) | ~$72.50 | Baseline |
| Late April (peak) | >$125.00 | 52-week high $126.10 |
| May 5 | ~$109.90 | Post-Fujairah attack |
| Jun 8 | $94.25 | Declining on deal hopes |
| Jun 15 | $83.63 | Post-deal announcement (−4.24% on day) |

- **US gasoline**: Rose from $2.98/gal (Feb 26) to $4.45/gal (May 3) — a **49% increase**
- **Global supply loss**: ~2 million barrels per day (IEA estimate)
- **US SPR release**: 17.5 million barrels (March–April)
- **Iran losses**: ~$435 million/day from US naval blockade
- **Goldman Sachs**: Estimated 30% US recession probability over 12 months
- **Q1 2026 US PCE inflation**: +4.5% annualized; Euro area energy inflation hit 10.9% in April

## 4. Ceasefire Deal Status (as of June 15–16, 2026)

- Trump announced June 14 that a deal is "complete" and the Strait will reopen.
- **Signing ceremony**: Scheduled **June 19, 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland**; VP JD Vance expected to attend.
- **Critical uncertainty**: The IRGC has **not endorsed** the deal. An Iranian lawmaker stated the Strait **"will never return to the status quo that existed before the war."**
- **Prediction markets**: Metaculus ~75% probability of formal signing before June 26; Polymarket ~81.5% probability by July 31.
- Insurance companies remain largely unwilling to cover Hormuz transit even under military escort.

## 5. OPEC & Supply Response

- **OPEC+ agreed** to a modest production increase of **188,000 bpd starting June 2026** — far insufficient to offset the ~2 million bpd Hormuz loss.
- **UAE departed OPEC** during the crisis.
- **Venezuela** stepped in as a significant offset: exports reached **1.23 million bpd** (7-year high); US imports from Venezuela reached **588,000 bpd** (week ending May 8).

## 6. Nuclear Dimension

- Iran's nuclear facilities at **Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan** were destroyed during Operation Midnight Hammer.
- DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified (March 18) that Iran's nuclear enrichment capabilities were destroyed.
- **Trump's 5 preconditions**: Iran must deliver 400 kg of enriched uranium to the US, maintain only one operational nuclear facility, and accept no war reparations.
- 52 US senators and 177 representatives signed a letter opposing any deal allowing continued Iranian enrichment.

## 7. Data Gaps

- No specific 2026 data was found on **Houthi Red Sea attacks**, which appear to have been overshadowed by the Hormuz crisis.
- Post-deal shipping restart timelines and insurance market conditions remain unclear.
